gears.comments: Grizzled Edge is best for a damaging Endmin build due to its high Physical DMG bonus scaling based on how many Vulnerable stack Endmin consumes during Crush with it being applied before the damage is dealt. When used correctly, Endmin's Combo Skill applying Originium Crystals will also ensure the buff effect will be 1.5x.

Swordmancer is a strong set for any Physical characters thanks to its Stagger Efficiency Bonus can allow teams to reach Stagger nodes faster. The set also adds an extra hit with extra Stagger and Physical DMG when triggering Physical Statuses through Endmin's Crushes.

Best 3P damage choice for Endmin by a small margin, which usually isn't enough to justify giving up the team utility Swordmancer provides, especially given you have to play around stagger windows in order to unlock its potential.Note: To get the listed results from this set, you'll need to make optimal use of stagger windows to take advantage of both kits having DMG% vs Stagger. If you can't, this set's performance will notably drop.

pros: Grants themself and the team ATK%.
Applies a sizable Physical damage taken debuff, benefitting themself and the team.
Can be played as either a primary damage dealer or support damage dealer.
Incredibly easy to trigger Combo Skill.
Improved SP efficiency when played as main damage dealer.
cons: Top performance requires careful play around Combo Skill Originium Crystals.
Can only apply Vulnerable to enemies who don’t already have it due to [Crush] being the only source of application.

gears.comments: Grizzled Edge is best for a damaging Endmin build due to its high Physical DMG bonus scaling based on how many Vulnerable stack Endmin consumes during Crush with it being applied before the damage is dealt. When used correctly, Endmin's Combo Skill applying Originium Crystals will also ensure the buff effect will be 1.5x.

Swordmancer is a strong set for any Physical characters thanks to its Stagger Efficiency Bonus can allow teams to reach Stagger nodes faster. The set also adds an extra hit with extra Stagger and Physical DMG when triggering Physical Statuses through Endmin's Crushes.

Best 3P damage choice for Endmin by a small margin, which usually isn't enough to justify giving up the team utility Swordmancer provides, especially given you have to play around stagger windows in order to unlock its potential.Note: To get the listed results from this set, you'll need to make optimal use of stagger windows to take advantage of both kits having DMG% vs Stagger. If you can't, this set's performance will notably drop.

pros: Grants themself and the team ATK%.
Applies a sizable Physical damage taken debuff, benefitting themself and the team.
Can be played as either a primary damage dealer or support damage dealer.
Incredibly easy to trigger Combo Skill.
Improved SP efficiency when played as main damage dealer.
cons: Top performance requires careful play around Combo Skill Originium Crystals.
Can only apply Vulnerable to enemies who don’t already have it due to [Crush] being the only source of application.

gears.comments: When using Snowshine for Cryo Infliction, her Skill has a cheap SP cost and can apply Protection to activate the Set Bonus. If you don't need the defensive utility from LYNX (especially if your team includes another Sustain) and there are no other viable holders of this set, then it is her best option.Note: This build is focused mainly on Ultimate Gain Efficiency first, Healing Output second. If you want to invest more into Healing Output or her personal damage, check out the alternative gears listed below.

Boosts the overall output of Snowshine's Healing while also granting her and the team a potent defensive 3P Set Bonus, which aids in the survival of the team. Running this set-up also fits in some additional energy for quicker Ultimate access.

pros: Combo has great up-front full team healing and applies a heal over time.
Ultimate forcibly applies Solidification in an AoE without the need to worry about Inflictions.
Combo can be activated in any team.
Using Battle Skill protects allies and parries enemy attacks, applying Cryo Infliction.
On-demand Protection from Battle Skill makes some support sets easy to maintain uptime on.
Successful parries with Battle Skill return a portion of the SP spent to use it.
She’s free!
cons: Despite the Combo being universally usable, it requires the team to be low on HP to trigger.
Cryo Infliction from Battle Skill isn’t instantly applied and is conditional, making it tough to rely on for core combos.

gears.comments: Eternal Xiranite is the go-to 3P set for all supports capable of activating its team-wide buffing effect and who can also benefit from the large amounts of Ultimate Gain each of its pieces provides. Ardelia applies Susceptibility frequently via her battle skill, which can trigger the 3P bonus, granted not as frequently or consistently as some other characters, meaning maximum uptime might need to be helped along by another Operator on this set as well. The INT, WILL and Ultimate Gain heavy pieces are all exactly what she's looking for from her gear.

pros: Applies long-duration Arts and Physical Susceptibility.
Incredibly generic and is able to buff most teams well.
Multiple sources of healing, capable of healing the controlled operator and the team.
Forcibly applies Corrosion, which can be useful to other characters.
Consumes Arts Reactions routinely, which can also be useful to some characters.
She's free!
Mr. Dolly.
cons: Can become locked out of Combo Skill in teams with lingering Infliction/Vulnerability stacks.
Requires up-front SP investment for her main damage buff.

gears.comments: Maximizing Perlica's Arts Intensity is the best way to boost her supportive capabilities among the available 3P set bonuses. Because of this, Pulser Labs is usually the best choice for her. Its gear pieces are also ideal for her playstyle, having the right stats as well as a lot of Arts Intensity. Last but not least its 3P active bonus grants her a large amount of personal damage as well.

Qingo gear pieces offer Will, Int, and most importantly Arts Intensity on its gear to amplify the Arts DMG Taken debuff from Electrification. The Combo Skill cooldown reduction can be useful in teams that consumes her Electrification debuff to enable their kits like with Zhuang Fangyi.

pros: Can forcibly apply Electrification for free with Combo Skill, boosting all Arts damage taken.
Can freely apply Electric Infliction quickly with the Battle Skill.
Above-average personal damage output for the support damage role.
Many viable, supportive weapon choices further boost support damage capabilities.
Incredibly easy to trigger Combo Skill usable in any team.
Might not always be optimal, but works in every Arts Damage-based team thanks to her generic combo and debuff.
She's free!
cons: Without potentials combo, Electrification has a low duration, which must be timed during large damage moments to get value out of.

gears.comments: Grizzled Edge is a damaging sets for characters that consumes Vulnerability stacks and Da Pan's best-in-slot when he can consistently achieve 4-stack Crushes. The set will also get stronger if the team includes Physical Susceptibility debuffs such as through Lifeng, Ardelia or others.

Swordmancer is the general best option for any Physical teams as the Stagger Efficiency Bonus can allow these teams to reach Stagger Nodes much faster. It increases all his Stagger output by 20% on top of adding an extra hit that deals 15 Stagger and some Physical DMG every 15s when he applies a Physical Status.

Aethertech has almost a full set of ideal set pieces for Da Pan, with all three stats being correct. This, combined with its powerful Set Bonus, which triggers on Vulnerable application (which Da Pan applies more than most other Operators do, thanks to his Ultimate) and its additional effect that triggers when applying up to 4 Vulnerable stacks (which Da Pan teams all want to achieve to trigger his combo) make it his standout choice. Other options could be considered for utility, but when it comes to damage, Aethertech is the go-to.

Type 50 Yinglung can allow a full build of matching Attributes for Da Pan and with lots of Physical DMG. The set offers permanent ATK increase and a massive DMG bonus to Combo Skills, but with the downside of lacking in Arts Intensity, a very strong stat for Da Pan, especially when he is dealing 4 stack Crushes.

pros: Free and boosted Crush application with Combo Skill, freeing up SP to use on Vulnerable applying Battle Skills.
Solid personal Physical DMG% buffs.
His stack application is above average as he applies 1 Vulnerable on Battle Skill and 2 with Ultimate.
Can lower the cooldown of Combo Skill significantly when played optimally, thanks to Salty or Mild talent.
cons: Cooldown-based Crush can sometimes leave scenarios where the team has Vulnerable ready but no spender.
Combo and main source of damage cannot be accessed without maxing out Vulnerable, demanding a more all-in Physical strategy than most.

gears.comments: Qingo is Chen's preferred option in pure Physical teams as the rotations tend to be gated by her Combo's Skill cooldown; the actual value of this set is greater than shown if properly utilized

Swordmancer is the general best option for any Physical teams as the Stagger Efficiency Bonus can allow these teams to reach Stagger Nodes much faster. It increases all Stagger output by 20% on top of adding an extra hit that deals 15 Stagger and some Physical DMG every 15s when she applies a Physical Status (which her Battle Skill or Combo Skill can).

pros: Free Vulnerability Infliction with combo.
Above average Stagger across abilities and talent.
Notable personal damage for a support damage dealer.
Incredibly easy to trigger Combo Skill in any Physical team.
Cheap Ultimate.
She’s free!
cons: Cannot consume the Vulnerability stacks she generates, forcing her to play with a character that can.

gears.comments: Akekuri's best set in almost all cases, as almost all of Froniters' pieces are ideal for her and the 3P bonus is an incredible boost to her supportive capabilities, allowing her to apply a DMG% buff to the team close to permanently. Also, as a bonus, the 3P also reduces the cooldown of her Combo Skill.

An alternative way to build Akekuri is to forego any set bonuses and opt for maximum Ultimate Gain Efficiency, allowing her to regenerate more SP for the entire team, and also provide ATK% and Link buff. Aim for pieces with Intelligence to maximize the SP recovery from her Combo Skill.

pros: Battle Skill applies Heat Infliction.
Can recover large amounts of SP through Ultimate and Combo Skill.
Combo Skill has a low cooldown which, if you Stagger enough, can be realistically utilized.
Strong supportive weapon and set options.
Grants Link, plus a small short-duration Attack boost when using Ultimate.
Very easy to get max Potentials.
cons: Higher than average Ultimate energy cost with no bonus energy generation.
Low damage compared to other Vanguards pushes her into a pure support role in most cases.

gears.comments: When playing Wulfgard as Heat Infliction applier for Laevatain, there are no good supportive gear options for him. As such, opting for Ultimate Gain Efficiency on all pieces can allow Wulfgard to trigger Laevatain's Combo Skill on demand more often, as it has a short cooldown of 9-10s. Recommended for when Wulfgard is not highly invested, since he won't be contributing much damage regardless.

Sub DPS option Wulfgard in a reaction based team where he is consistently applying Combustion through Arts Reactions or Ultimate. Arts Intensity results in strong Combustion DMG and is his best choice when supporting Rossi

Qingo can reduce his Combo Skill cooldown by ~3s, allowing for more Heat Infliction, while also significantly improving his personal DMG across all abiliites. Can also replace any gear piece with more Ultimate Gain Efficiency if needed.

pros: Free AoE Heat Infliction application via Combo Skill.
Combo Skill is easy to trigger by himself and in any Arts Infliction team.
Can forcibly apply Combustion in an AoE.
Capable of playing as a support damage dealer or main damage dealer.
Can quickly inflict Heat with Battle Skill if SP is available.
cons: No supportive capabilities outside of Heat Infliction and Combustion at P0.
Battle skill, either used as a large damage nuke OR Heat Infliction - cannot do both.
Requires a constant stream of Electrification or Combustion to consume as a DPS.

gears.comments: Running Hot Work with Redeemer Armor is the recommended build on Laevatain, as the extra Ultimate Gain Efficiency will guarantee Laevatain has her Ultimate after 2 rotations of Enhanced Battle Skill plus Combo Skill. The build offers massive boosts to Battle Skill DMG, which has value when you set up her Enhanced Battle Skills with buffs/debuffs. The build overall is a slight damage loss when compared to LYNX Slab, but it will offer more flexibility in her rotations, especially in faster-paced teams that prefer cycling through Ultimates as fast as possible.

Hot Work with LYNX Slab is the most damage-optimized build for a highly invested Laevatain (Maxed Weapon Essence, Gear Artificing, Attribute Nodes).  Despite LYNX Slab only having 1 matching Attribute, it offers Main Attribute % - a stat she cannot gain from anywhere else and it multiplies her Intellect, resulting in the highest ATK possible. If you do not have a maxed out build, with low Intellect, it will result in damage loss instead due to not having enough Attributes to multiply.  If you don't need the extra Ultimate Energy from Tidefall Armor it will be her best option, for example in some teams it may take up to 3 Combo Skills to get her Ultimate - which is common if your're running teams with many buffs/debuffs that need reapplying, then Laevatain won't usually need additional Energy as SP recovery is a greater limiting factor.


1.1 Note: The new Exo-Rig Armor is a game changer and now LYNX Slab beats other builds in damage by around 10%. Run this build if you can play around base Energy gain, the damage difference is much more noticable.

Requires Gear Artificing to bring her Ultimate Gain Efficiency up to near max and is only recommended with her Signature Weapon. Running no set, full Ultimate Gain Efficiency to double her Energy gain is an unconventional but valid way to build Laevatain, as it has all matching Attributes for her and it can allow Laevatain to immediately regain her Ultimate back after using Enhanced Battle Skill + Combo Skill + some team Battle Skills. Although you will trade more than half of your Enhanced Battle Skill DMG for better Ultimate uptime. Laevatain can focus on unleashing her Enhanced BATKs which is massively boosted by her Signature Weapon.

pros: Huge AoE damage.
Outstanding total damage output.
Gains substantially better access to energy and enhanced skills in AoE encounters.
Has a cheat death talent when dropping below 40% HP.
cons: Ultimate reliant.
Poor weapon choices outside of signature and battle pass.
Cannot use the finisher during Ultimate as the main field operator.

gears.comments: Best set for Arclight in most teams after factoring in the buffs it provides to her teammates, even if wearing it results in a major reduction of her own personal damage. What makes it strong is the 3P bonus, which grants a DMG% buff to the whole team almost permanently thanks to Arclight's Combo spam. The other benefit of this set is its focus on boosting Secondary Attributes, something which may or may not be impactful to your Arclight depending on how optimized your gameplay is. In teams where you align her talent Wildland Trekker, with your team's burst, investing heavily into Intellect makes sense, but if you're not specifically timing it, boosting Arclight's own damage can be stronger.

If you're playing Arclight in a more damage-focused role and are fine giving up the team-based benefits of Frontiers, Bonekrusha will grant her more personal damage thanks to its damage-focused 3P and superior damage choices on gear pieces.Note: To get the listed results out of this set, you'll need to make optimal use of Stagger windows to take advantage of both kits having DMG% vs Stagger. If you can't, this set’s performance will notably drop.

pros: Low Combo Skill cooldown, making for constant buff uptime on items that demand SP recovery.
Frequent Combo and Battle Skill usage leads to a lot of stagger application, which can help with buff alignment in certain rotations.
SP Recovery on her Battle Skill makes her an effective Ultimate Energy battery.
Provides both Electric Infliction and Electrification with her Ultimate, easing skill use requirements for certain teams and rotations.
Always primed for optimal performance.
cons: Provides weaker buffs for Electric teams than her peers, needs secondary, not primary building to maximise it, and has split damage types diminishing her personal contribution.
Is a net SP drain on teams and consumes Electrification - one of the strongest reactions - making resource management and buff alignment difficult.
Ultimate Electrification is often worse than those of other Operators owing to low Arts Intensity, demanding more complex rotations.
Excessively reliant on all of her Potentials, needing all 5 to truly have comfortable rotations.
The wind never stops, so you better keep up.

gears.comments: A set that focuses entirely on Battle Skill damage, making it an ideal fit for Avywenna, as that's where a huge portion of her damage comes from. Has a great 3P set effect as well as excellent gear pieces, making it the de facto pick unless you need something else very specific.

pros: Very short time needed for said burst, with forgiving buff alignment needs.
Striker that does not consume Inflictions, making reaction management easy.
Flexible team options, needing only Electric inflictions to function.
She is, indeed, looking alright from this angle.
cons: Significant set-up needed for her burst, sometimes even needing to delay attacks.
Requires good positioning for her skills to land, being an even bigger problem on smaller enemies.
Overly reliant on Potentials for best performance.
Awful Basic Attack sequence, demanding occasional Operator swaps.
A pretty girl like her has gotten a lot of gifts, so you better make it special.

gears.comments: Eternal Xiranite is the go-to 3P set for all supports capable of activating its team-wide buffing effect and who can also benefit from the large amounts of Ultimate Gain each of its pieces provides. Gil is only able to activate the 3P bonus of this set with her Ultimate, but each of this set's gear pieces is strong enough that there's no other set she'd rather run. Because she's not great at triggering the bonus, bringing other operators who wear it is a good way to improve uptime.

Swordmancer has niche use cases on Gilberta thanks to her Combo Skill forcibly applying Lift and activating the set effect. Make sure you have enough Ultimate Gain Efficiency either through her signature weapon or gear pieces to ensure your Ultimate is ready for burst windows. Swordmancer increases her Stagger output by 20% on top of the extra hit, and the pieces with Arts Intensity can further increase the debuff potency of her Corrosion in some teams that trigger it.

pros: On-demand AoE enemy gathering through Battle Skill.
Nature Infliction application on Battle Skill and Ultimate.
Damage buffing potential between Ultimate Susceptibility, weapon, and gear choices.
Inflicts above average Stagger for a support.
Increases Ultimate Gain of specific class types, helping with energy breakpoints.
Many long-duration crowd control effects.
cons: Combo skill requires Arts Reactions, which isn’t something every team can consistently do.
Main damage buffing, her Ultimate has an incredibly low duration.
Crowd Control and Gathering effects are not usable against some enemy types.
Healing requires at least 2 targets to work.
Battle skill is a channeled ability, meaning the Infliction and damage it causes are delayed.

gears.comments: Swordmancer is a drop for Lifeng's personal damage due to having less optimal damage gear pieces, but its 3P Set Bonus has a much better supportive effect, which, if you're ok giving up some of Lifeng's personal output, can be worth the trade off. The 3P bonus of Swordmancer grants an on-hit effect that deals 10 Stagger every 15 seconds and increases all Stagger Lifeng outputs by 20%. It's important to keep in mind, though, in order to activate Swordmancer's on-hit effect, Lifeng must apply a Physical Status, which his opening Battle Skill against non-Vulnerable enemies doesn’t. This means you'll need to weave in additional Battle Skills while the enemy is already Vulnerable or rely on his Ultimate to trigger it instead.Note: With Swordmancer, our calculations don't reach a new stagger breakpoint, but if they allowed for an additional one over Bonekrusha, its overall team damage would be higher. This will also be the case in your own play; if Swordmancer gets an extra stagger, it can be better, if it doesn't, it can fall behind.

When using Lifeng's Battle Skill as the opener to apply Vulnerbility and Physical Susceptibility, it can activate the Set Effect to provide a teamwide buff, which can result in overall higher team damage at the cost of his personal damage. This build has some Ultimate Gain Efficiency for Lifeng which allows him to apply more Vulnerable stacks, and his Combat Talent allows any Attribute to be converted to ATK.

pros: Easily applies Physical Susceptibility at the same time as Vulnerability.
Grants Link to the team,
Applies a large amount of Vulnerability through Ultimate.
Gains value from all stats, not just his main and secondary.
Outputs a strong amount of Stagger.
Activates his own combo.
cons: Cannot consume the Vulnerability stacks he generates, forcing him to play with a character that can.
Can’t activate his damage talent, Subduer of Evil, on every Knock Down without giving up his Physical Susceptibility without potentials.
Battle Skill has a long animation, which can leave Lifeng vulnerable.
Timing Combo Skill to apply Link to the right ability takes planning and proper execution to do well.

gears.comments: Despite not having perfect gear pieces with ideal stats for Alesh, Frontiers is still his best set in most teams thanks to its 3P Set Bonus, which allows him to grant the squad a DMG% boost he can easily maintain throughout the fight. Also, while the gear pieces aren't optimal for dealing damage with him, they all have a large amount of Intellect, which is powerful for boosting his attribute scaling talent.
Alesh can opt for no set aiming for full Ultimate Gain Efficiency build while still having high Intellect from the pieces to improve his Combat Talent.

pros: Can forcibly apply Solidification through exclusively Cryo Infliction (not normally possible).
Battle skill can return a large portion of the SP spent on it.
Generates bonus SP for the team through Combo and Ultimate.
Cryo Infliction on Ultimate.
Can grant even more SP when getting lucky with combo.
Strong supportive weapon and set options.
Low Combo Skill cooldown compared to others that can actually be taken advantage of.
cons: Sometimes you get unlucky...
No buffing outside of sets and weapons before unlocking P3.

gears.comments: Boosts the overall output of Ember's healing while also granting her and the team a potent defensive 3P Set Bonus, which aids in the survival of the team. Running this setup also fits in some additional energy for quicker Ultimate access.Note: Maxing out sustain isn't the only way to play Ember, so if you're after something else more specific, there are likely better options.

pros: Can apply Vulnerable without needing to spend SP via Combo Skill.
Applies team-wide shielding based on own HP.
Easy to trigger Combo Skill-based healing for the controlled operator.
Gains Protection when using Battle or Combo skill, which activates support set effects.
The Combo Skill trigger requirement is completely generic and can be activated in any team.
cons: Despite having an easy-to-trigger Combo Skill, it can sometimes be inconvenient to activate.
The Combo Skill does not heal when it misses enemies.
No damage boosting potential outside of sets and weapons.

gears.comments: 3P Eternal Xiranite is the go-to option for consistency when playing Xaihi. It grants her the most frequent Ultimates and a powerful team-wide buff that she can easily keep active for the majority of the fight. With that said, it's not her best set if all you want is maximum damage output. If that's what you're after, playing into Xaihi's Ultimate's Intellect scaling may yield better results, but will come at the cost of multiple energy breakpoints, leading to stronger but less frequent Ultimates.

Since the Eternal Xiranite buff cannot stack, some Cryo teams maybe already include another ally that can reliably activate this effect. Instead, Xalhi's Ultimate gains additional Amp per Intellect, so running no set may be optimal in some team compositions as each pieces have Secondary Attribute % which multiplies your Intellect Attribute. The biggest trade-off is that you cannot run as much Ultimate Gain Efficiency, and you miss out on any set bonuses.

pros: Offers a large and consistent amount of Amp in addition to other, more minor bonuses.
Good supportive weapon and gear choices, which further boost team damage buffing.
Consistent and accessible healing for the controlled operator without needing to adjust gameplay.
Cryo Infliction with Combo Skill.
Battle skill buff can be used before combat to save SP in some encounters.
cons: Some buffs rely on being at or healing to maximum HP to be applied.
Major buffs, Cryo Infliction, and healing are all tied to landing multiple Final Strikes.

gears.comments: Due to Estella's main stat of Will being somewhat unique among Physical Damage Dealers at the time of release, her Set options aren't as varied or flexible as those of other Physical scalers. Because of this, Type 50 Yinlung is her best choice even if it doesn't offer any supportive capabilities.

pros: Very easy to get max potentials.
Cryo Infliction on Battle Skill.
Battle skill becomes more SP efficient after consuming Shatter.
Inflicts Vulnerable with Ultimate and Combo Skill.
Applies Physical Susceptibility with Ultimate.
cons: Damage is split between Physical and Cryo, making scaling less efficient.
Combo needs Solidification to work something that doesn’t synergize with the Physical archetype she primarily supports.
Has a strong supportive kit for Physical teams, but the combo requirement forces a mix of Cryo plus Physical, which makes team build less flexible and usually just weaker than pure variants.

gears.comments: MI Security is currently the best set for Yvonne thanks to the itemization of its gear pieces, favoring Basic ATK DMG% plus Crit Rate and its 3P effect being easy to trigger and maintain through Yvonne's usually high Crit and very high hit count across abilities. However, if you're experiencing ramp-up issues with the 3P buff effect or are having trouble keeping it active during the majority of the fight, investing more heavily into Crit in gear piece options will help. The recommended gear configuration focuses on a balance between Crit and Basic DMG% as a baseline, but if you have a specific goal in mind, we recommend changing out pieces so the load-out fits your playstyle exactly. Check the list of gear alternatives for ideas of how to do this.

pros: Large amounts of burst damage during Ultimate.
Can play with Nature or Cryo Infliction, boosting teammate options.
Consistently Solidifies enemies, disabling non-boss targets, aiding in survivability.
cons: Enemies can move out of Combo skill, resulting in potentially large losses of damage and Solidification.
Ultimate relies on a long channel time which, if interfered with, will cost Yvonne a lot of damage.
Can waste energy, infliction stacks, and potential, if energy isn’t planned around.
Requires Infliction but cannot apply it herself, meaning she must rely on teammates.

gears.comments: Despite being a Defender, Catcher isn't able to use the premier defensive 3P set LYNX due to him not being able to heal. Instead, the next best thing to equip him with is either Xiranite and full Energy to maximize his ability to apply Vulnerable and Weaken while also occasionally buffing the team. Alternatively, you can put investment into extra Stagger with Swordmancer. In most cases, we've found the extra Energy to be more impactful, but Swordmancer is also there as a choice if you'd prefer it.

pros: Applies Weaken and Vulnerability with Ultimate.
Using Battle Skill protects allies and parries enemy attacks, applying Vulnerability.
On-demand Protection from Battle Skill makes some support sets easy to maintain uptime on.
Very easy to get the max potentials of.
Battle Skill returns a portion of the SP spent to use it.
Combo applies a shield to self and the controlled operator.
cons: No healing, even as a Defender class operator.
Combo has a very long cooldown when compared to other operators.

gears.comments: In teams focused on stacking Arts Infliction, her Ultimate can add 1 extra stack. As she currently cannot run any other sets that provide buffs, building full Ultimate Gain Efficiency is recommended.

As Fluorite is currently being used in the meta, she has two different Set paths she can go down, Ultimate gain to access her Ultimate-based Arts Inflcition sooner or Combo Skill reduction to target, making her Combo more accessible. Both are viable, and with Frontiers, you can still get a good amount of Ultimate gain from a flex piece and Weapon, giving you a slightly faster Ultimate and Combo Skill. For those dedicated, it's also possible to build Fluorite with a focus on damage, but Infliction is usually more valuable.

pros: Battle Skill applies on-demand Nature Infliction.
Combo Skill and Ultimate can apply either Nature or Cryo Infliction.
Very easy to get max potentials of.
cons: Combo skill has a long cooldown, which also hurts Ultimate access, limiting both sources of free Inflictions.
Battle Skill is delayed, requiring a short wait before Infliction, and damage is triggered.
Not able to provide any damage boosting support to the team.

gears.comments: Currently, Tide Surge is the only 3P set worth considering running on Last Rite, the reason being that its set pieces are simply too well-tailored to her stat needs. When considering gear pieces for Last Rite, depending on what you're after, you can either prioritize Ultimate Gain in order to achieve the fastest Ultimates possible or more damage to maximize burst. Which is right for you will depend on how many inflictions you're consuming per Combo Skill to access Last Rite's Ultimate and if you're targeting any major energy breakpoints in order to skip Combo Skills completely.

pros: Large amounts of burst damage.
Cryo Susceptibility, which boosts her own damage and that of other Cryo teammates.
SP efficient Cryo Infliction application.
Can use Battle Skill pre-combat to gain free SP in encounters that allow it.
Multiple Basic Attack Cancels/Tricks, which help address cons and boost performance.
cons: Has ramp-up time if Ultimate isn’t pre-prepared earlier.
Long animation times.
Can waste energy, Cryo Infliction stacks, and potential, if energy isn’t planned around.
Rotation is hindered in teams that consume Inflictions for other purposes.

gears.comments: Eternal Xiranite is the go-to 3P set for all supports capable of activating its team-wide buffing effect and who can also benefit from the large amounts of Ultimate Gain each of its pieces provides. Antal can trigger the 3P bonus effect via his Battle Skill every 60 seconds and his Ultimate whenever it becomes available. Because his application of Susceptibility and Amp are more spread out and long-lasting, he isn't the best at keeping this set up at all times, meaning running another support on it at the same time as him isn't a bad idea. The INT, WILL and Ultimate Gain heavy pieces are all fairly well suited for what Antal is looking for in gear.

pros: Very easy to get max potentials.
Applies Heat/Electric Susceptibility close to permanently on your chosen target with Battle Skill.
Applies Heat/Electric Amp to the whole team with Ultimate.
Strong supportive weapon and set options.
Combo skill can apply any of the elemental Infliction or Vulnerability to your target.
Inbuilt healing to help with keeping the team alive.
cons: Susceptibility from Battle Skill is only on a single target.
In fights with mostly weak enemies that die quickly, maintaining Focus can be SP-intensive.

gears.comments: Qingbo set can increase all of Tangtang's Skill DMG - both Battle Skill and Ultimate with more focus on Battle Skill from the gear pieces. Reduces her Combo Skill cooldown by ~2s, allowing for more Whirlpool generation.

Bonekrusha is the recommended build for an invested Tangtang as it has matching Attributes and ideal stats (Cryo DMG & Battle Skill DMG) to enable her strong Sub-DPS capabilities.
Note: Some Ultimate Gain Effiiciency is recommended in fights that start with 0 Energy since her Ultimate is 90 Energy without additional help through her kit, and sometimes SP Return can intefere with your Energy gains.

MI Security is a competitive option that can sometimes beat out Bonekrusha in terms of damage but may be incosistent due to low 10% Crit Rate. Maintaining stacks is not difficult on Tangtang due to her BATK and Battle Skill causing multiple small hits to stack up the buff. The main advantage over Bonekrusha is that MI Security can increase the DMG of Watersprouts from her Ultimate while Bonekrusha does not.

Alternatively for low investment Tangtang, Eternal Xiranite is a great option as the DMG% buff can be activated through her Battle Skill's Susceptibility debuff, althrough the buff cannot be stacked and most of her teams commonly include an ally that can reliably activate this set. The advantage of this set is the large Ultimate Gain Efficiency boost allowing more frequent access to her Ultimate.

pros: Flexible unit who can fit multiple roles: she can do fine as a main-DPS or as a sub-DPS, and can be built either with damage or supportive sets.
Jack of all trades, master of all: has a high damage ceiling, relatively cheap Cryo Inflictions, buffs the team, etc.
Strong utility owing to the wide range on her moves and the absolute immobilize effect on her Ultimate that can even hold Elites/Bosses in place.
Tangtang certificates can be exchanged for favors from the boss.
cons: Has a notable wind-up time to get the most value out of her Susceptibility, demanding multiple Combo Skill triggers to build-up to it.
Somewhat dependent on her signature weapon for the best buffs, as alternatives are for personal damage and/or stats only.
The need for a Dive Attack during her Ultimate can make it awkward to activate in burst periods with certain Operators.
The above cons are false. The Supreme Chief obviously has no faults.

gears.comments: MI Security is the go-to option for crit-scaling Operators as the gears have a lot of Critical Rate for them to work with, alongside the set bonus providing a nice 25% ATK and 10% Critical Rate if the wielder can consistently score critical hits. Rossi needs Critical Rate as her kit provides an additional Critical DMG bonus in her abilities, and she deals additional hits through her Combat Talent when scoring critical hits. The gear pieces also have her preferred Attributes of Agility and Intellect.

pros: High damage ceiling, having a strong Ultimate burst and good multipliers on both Combo & Battle Skills, making for a well-rounded DPS.
Can work in a variety of teams, with valid options for both Arts and Physical compositions.
Easy to gear, with plenty of competitive weapon choices aside from her signature.
Nella botte piccola, c'e il vino buono.
cons: Split damage types makes team-building awkward, with anti-synergies across different members.
Can have long and/or SP-demanding rotations depending on team and content type (e.g. 0 vs full Energy starts).
Perfect timing Combo Skill is non-negotiable to get the most out of the character, which some may find overbearing.
In bocca al lupo...

gears.comments: Frontiers is a set that can be easily activated by Pog through his Combo Skill, making it one of his more generic and flexible options. However, the stats on his best pieces are still all over the place with offensive stats through Physical DMG from Kits but major stats mismatch on the armor results in low Attributes. Frontiers is neither his best damaging or supporting option; but it is recommended due to the ease of usage in any of his teams. For those with optimized Physical team rotations it is recommended to check his other sets.

Swordmancer is a set that is only valuable on Breach Pog, as the set requires him to trigger a Physical Status. Swordmancer increases all his Stagger output on top of adding a hit with 10 Stagger can allow enemies to be Staggered quicker. The pieces also offer Arts Intensity which is his most valuable stat; can further amplify the Physical DMG Taken debuff from Breach, which is ideal in Physical teams where Pog is Breaching with high Vulnerability stacks, notably against Bosses where you can take advantage of the long debuff duration from Breach.
For Pure Breach support Pog, Hot Works can provide the highest Arts Intensity thanks to the 3-pc bonus providing an additional 30. These gears also have a decent amount of Will for his own damage.

pros: Applies Breach efficiently with Battle Skill.
Gains additional benefits the more Vulnerability he or the team consumes.
His Ultimate has a large AoE that gathers enemies.
SP Recovery capabilities on every ability.
Solid buffs across talents, weapons, and gear choices.
cons: Loses potential when the team isn’t consuming maximum Vulnerability stacks each rotation.
Must decide between Breach or Crush in teams that have both when consuming Vulnerability stacks.

gears.comments: Xiranflow is a set designed for Zhuang Fangyi with all her desired Attributes and stats (Will, Intellect, Battle Skill / Electric DMG). The set provides her with 10% ATK and up to 45% Electric DMG Dealt at max stacks, which is obtained from consuming Arts Reaction. The stack amount is based on the debuff's Status Level, so consuming Lv. 2 Electrification will immediately grant 2 stacks. All standard Zhuang Fangyi rotations will effortlessly maintain 2 stacks, with moments of 3 stacks occurring briefly in her pre-Ultimate rotations and constantly during her Ultimate.

pros: Incredibly strong all-around: can manage both high sustained damageandburst, has great range on attacks and even high mobility.
Inexpensive teams, with strong performance even on a full line-up of 4 to 5-star Operators.
Ultimate install duration makes for extended burst periods, being especially useful in multi-wave stages.
Blessed the world with Chubby Lungs.
cons: Very SP/Infliction hungry, with long ramp-up periods for Sunderblades, leading to slow rotations and delayed burst.
Few low-cost options for her weapon, with a significant gap between her Signature and alternatives.
Locked out of Finishers during her Ultimate state (which is very long!), making for awkward rotations and stagger alignment.
Not edible.

gears.comments: Grizzled Edge is best for a damaging Endmin build due to its high Physical DMG bonus scaling based on how many Vulnerable stack Endmin consumes during Crush with it being applied before the damage is dealt. When used correctly, Endmin's Combo Skill applying Originium Crystals will also ensure the buff effect will be 1.5x.

Swordmancer is a strong set for any Physical characters thanks to its Stagger Efficiency Bonus can allow teams to reach Stagger nodes faster. The set also adds an extra hit with extra Stagger and Physical DMG when triggering Physical Statuses through Endmin's Crushes.

Best 3P damage choice for Endmin by a small margin, which usually isn't enough to justify giving up the team utility Swordmancer provides, especially given you have to play around stagger windows in order to unlock its potential.Note: To get the listed results from this set, you'll need to make optimal use of stagger windows to take advantage of both kits having DMG% vs Stagger. If you can't, this set's performance will notably drop.

pros: Grants themself and the team ATK%.
Applies a sizable Physical damage taken debuff, benefitting themself and the team.
Can be played as either a primary damage dealer or support damage dealer.
Incredibly easy to trigger Combo Skill.
Improved SP efficiency when played as main damage dealer.
cons: Top performance requires careful play around Combo Skill Originium Crystals.
Can only apply Vulnerable to enemies who don’t already have it due to [Crush] being the only source of application.
